texts.full_text: early 1980s, Grant used her lunch hour to join an anti-apartheid protest in downtown Toronto. “I remember some of my work colleagues passing by and asking what I was doing there,” recalled Grant who was a member of the province’s anti-apartheid movement. “It was also a time when the Employment Standards Act didn’t protect cleaners, so I also spent lunch hours with Portuguese women protesting the lack of protection. Then is when it occurred to me that I needed to go to law school.” Called to the Bar in 1989 after graduating from Osgoode Hall Law School two years earlier, Grant started a solo practice restricted to labour and human rights law and charter litigation and held a number of policy and legal positions in the public sector, including labour policy analyst, occupational health and safety prosecutor and counsel to the Pay Equity Tribunal and the Human Rights Board of Inquiry. There was one case that stood out about 25 years ago when she was an occupational health and safety crown and she had to upbraid a female police officer for presenting a drug case without evidence. “I went to the hearing and met the officer, who was a small, blonde woman about 5’ 2”, about an hour in advance,” recounted Grant. “When I asked where the evidence is, she looked at me and said, ‘Well, he was a big Black guy’. I thought she was going to elaborate. What she wanted to convey to me was that she felt intimidated and she had to slap a charge on him. She couldn’t tell me about anything he said or did. I told her to leave and come back with her ‘A’ game. She did exactly that, saying we need to withdraw the charge.” Grant also represented various equity-seeking organizations in appellate charter litigation, including the Legal Education and Action Fund (LEAF), the Coalition of Visible Minority Women (Ontario) Inc. and the African Canadian Legal Clinic. This becomes even more critical when dealing with potentially dangerous or large animals that might pose risks to humans due to their size, strength, or unpredictable behavior. Moreover, environmental conditions such as terrain, weather, and visibility can further complicate training and monitoring efforts. An operator in close proximity may be hindered by these factors, making it difficult to maintain control and observe the animal's well-being consistently. To address these challenges, there is a need for an apparatus that enables remote operation and monitoring of animals. Such a system would allow operators to guide and communicate with animals from a safe distance, reducing risk while enhancing training efficiency and safety. The invention described herein seeks to provide solutions through control environmental sensors, and communication interfaces tailored specifically for use in animal management. The core features of the proposed apparatus include: 1. Robotic Control Mechanism: This component allows an operator to remotely direct the movements and actions of an animal using a range of controls such as joysticks or touchscreens. The control signals are transmitted wirelessly, ensuring flexibility and safety for both the operator and the animal. 2. Environmental Monitoring System: Integrated sensors collect data on environmental conditions around the animal, including temperature, humidity, and terrain analysis. This information helps ensure that training sessions occur under optimal conditions and can alert operators to hazards or stressors affecting the animal's 3. Communication Interface: A two-way communication system enables real-time interaction between the operator and the animal through auditory signals (such as whistles or tones) and possibly visual cues like LED indicators

texts.full_text: young campers. The program provides flexibility and the needs of each camper are taken into account. Your child will quickly feel she is part of the Tanamakoon family and will want toreturn every year to participate in the camp which provides life long experience forthe campers. Campers live in comfortable wood cabins or platform tents. Younger campers have live in counselors. Accommodation placement is determined in consultation with the camper families in advance of the camp season. From time to time special trips are arranged for the older campers outside of the boundaries of Algonquin Park. These excursions are called tripping and include mountain biking, kayaking and canoe trips to the Ottawa and French rivers. Daily activities for younger campers are structured so that they can obtain a foundation of camping skills. Programs are filled with exciting new learning experiences and skill development. Older campers can deviate from standard programs to allow for individual interests. All activities are planned and supervised by trained Counselors who pay attention to the needs of each child. Regular activities include such things as environmental studies, wood crafting, racquet sports, mountain biking, climbing wall, archery, fitness, swimming, canoeing, kayaking, sailing, windsurfing, arts & crafts, live theatre acting, pottery, music and miniature horse care - just to name a few. Swimming classes include Lifesaving and Red Cross instruction. A safety system is in place to ensure your child's camp experience will be safe and accident free. Camp Tanamakoon will provide your daughter with an exceptional experience that she can apply to situations and challenges that may arise throughout childhood development and adult life. texts.full_text: isolated from urinary tract and wound infections from Pakistan (n=25). Isolates were subjected to commercially available microarray analysis to determine the presence of ESBLs and acquired AmpC enzymes. The genetic diversity of the isolates was determined by pulsed-field gel electrophoresis (PFGE) and multilocus sequence typing (MLsT). Plasmid replicon typing and capsular serotyping were conducted by PCR. Finally, screening for virulent factors and methylase genes was performed by PCR. The study found that all isolates were positive for CTX-M ESBLs, with CTX-M-15 being the most prevalent variant. Interestingly, 92% of the isolates harbored the 16S rRNA methylase gene , which confers resistance to aminoglycosides. This high prevalence of among ESBL-producing is a cause for concern, as it can contribute to multi-drug resistance and limit treatment options. Genetic diversity analysis revealed 18 distinct PFGE patterns and 14 sequence types (STs) among the isolates, indicating a diverse population of causing infections in Islamabad. The most prevalent ST was ST15, which has been associated with healthcare-associated infections globally. The majority of isolates belonged to capsular serotypes K1, K2, and K5, which are known to be highLy virulent. Additionally, several virulence factors were detected, including genes for aerobactin, yersiniabactin, and colibactin production. This study highlights the need for continuous surveillance of ESBL-producing in Pakistan, as these pathogens can cause severe infections and have limited treatment options due to their resistance profiles. texts.full_text: o many debates, discussions, and abuses. We set up a general model and list many of the terms affecting parallel processing. We scrutinize the validity of neglecting certain terms in different approximations, with special emphasis on the famous scaling laws of parallel processing. We clarify that when using the right interpretation of terms, Amdahl’s Law is the governing law of all kinds of parallel processing. Amdahl’s Law describes among others the history of supercomputing, the inherent performance limitation of the different kinds of parallel processing methods and the performance expectations for future parallel systems. Furthermore, we discuss how Amdahl's Law provides insights into the trade-offs between single-thread performance and the number of threads, highlighting the importance of balancing these factors to achieve optimal overall system performance. We also explore the implications of Amdahl’s Law for heterogeneous computing environments, where different types of processors work together, showing how the law can be applied to evaluate the effectiveness of such systems. Lastly, we address common misconceptions and pitfalls relAted to the application of Amdahl's Law, emphasizing the need for careful consideration of all terms and their interrelations to avoid flawed conclusions. texts.full_text: have proved to be useful tools in CR geometry, providing a way to study the geometry of CR manifolds using the well-developed theory of principal bundles and differential forms. The canonical Cartan connection in CR geometry was first constructed by Cartan himself in his seminal work on pseudoconvex hypersurfaces in ℂ², and later generalized by Tanaka and Chern-Moser to higher dimensions. However, the traditional approach to defining the canonical Cartan connection in CR geometry is abstract and algebraically intensive, involving prolongation procedures and the manipulation of abstract jet bundles. In this paper, we aim to provide a more direct and geometrically intuitive definition by working explicitly with the differential geometry of the CR manifold itself. Our approach is inspired by the work of Šilhan and others on the Fefferman space of a CR manifold, which is a circle bundle equipped with a canonical conformal Lorentzian structure. By working with the Fefferman space and the canonical conformal structure, we are able to give a simple and explicit construction of the Cartan bundle and connection, making the theory more accessible and providing new insights into the geometry of CR manifolds. The structure of the paper is as follows: In Section 2, we review the basic definitions and properties of CR manifolds and their tangential Cauchy-Riemann complexes. We also introduce the Fefferman space of a CR manifold and discuss its canonical conformal structure. texts.full_text: e, the moon glows like an ethereal spotlight over the undulating landscape below. The subtle interplay between light and darkness creates a serene atmosphere that seems to invite viewers into its quietude. It's no wonder Leong compares it to heaven; Robinson has painted not just a scene but also evoked emotion. Born in Queensland, Australia, William Robinson (1936-present) is widely acclaimed for his distinctive interpretations of the Australian landscape—specifically those featuring vast skies and towering trees. His use of light infuses each painting with an almost mystical aura, turning even mundane sCenes into something extraordinary. His latest exhibition showcases more than sixty works spanning over four decades – from early realistic paintings inspired by European masters like Van Gogh to his later semi-abstraction phase where he began exploring spiritual themes deeply rooted in Aboriginal culture. Through it all, there remains a consistent fascination with the natural world around him and its power to inspire reverence. But what makes Robinson's landscapes truly exceptional is how they transcend mere representation of physical reality; instead offering personal perspectives on nature as seen through his unique creative lens. He doesn't just capture images but also translates them into something deeply felt: tranquil moments stolen from daily life, infused with nostalgia for childhood experiences or profound insights gained during years spent living and travelling across this continent. If you find yourself drawn to 'Blue Moon', it may be because Robinson has invited us not merely witness his artistic prowess but share in these personal reflections too. For isn't art at its core about sharing a glimpse into another soul? And doesn't the Australian landscape possess infinite potential for such encounters, when rendered by hands as skilled and spiritually attuned those of William Robinson? texts.full_text: ry to use them as actual tools to open jars with, relieve headache, or cope with a difficult human being. They are pretty. Pretty like a dress you wear on banner days when you yourself feel gorgeous and all the world is right. But most of our lives - of my life, anyway - didn't happen in the way of lace and poetry and kid gloves. It happened with bitten nails and chapped lips, screaming alarm clocks, and much weariness. Synchronized visibility: Yes, synchronizing the access to an object using a lock can ensure that all threads see the most recent version of its members, even if they have been edited by other threads. This is because the happens-before relationship established at the end (release) of the lock ensures that any changes made to the object after the lock was acquirEd are visible to all threads that acquire the same lock afterwards. 2. Volatile objects and CPU caches: If an object reference change is marked as volatile, it means that the JVM will ensure that any changes made to the object's state are immediately visible to all threads, even if they have cached a copy of the old object reference in their CPU cach

SuperPower Inc., plans to use this licensing agreement as an opportunity for developing commercial applications. They will develop a prototype that demonstrates the feasibility of using tape-based wires in electric motors and generators. The license also allows SuperPower to produce high current cables with low electrical resistance (less than 1 ohm per meter) at liquid nitrogen temperatures (-196°C). The company plans on building an industrial-size production line for these superconducting tapes. SuperPower Inc. has already demonstrated the feasibility of using tape-based wires in electric motors and generators by producing a prototype that can be used to power large wind turbines or other renewable energy sources, as well as high-performance magnets needed for medical imaging equipment such as MRI machines. The Lab's technology could also lead to more efficient transmission lines with lower electrical resistance. This would reduce the amount of electricity lost during long-distance transmissions and make it possible to transmit larger amounts of power over longer distances without increasing costs. In addition, this licensing agreement will allow SuperPower Inc., to develop fault current controllers that can prevent lightning strikes from burning out equipment in transmission lines. texts.full_text: he focus is on the conduct following the termination. The Sherman Anti-Trust Act claim centers around allegations that the defendant engaged in practices constituting an unlawful restraint of trade. To succeed on this claim under § 1 or § 2 of the Sherman Act, the plaintiff must demonstrate a conspiracy among competitors to restrain trade (§ 1) or monopolistic behavior by the defendant intended to maintain or acquire market power (§ 2). In cases involving vertical relationships like distributorships, courts often consider whether the actions amount to an unreasonable restraint on competition. For the interference with contractual relations claim, it must be shown that the defendant intentionally induced a party not to perform under a contract with the plaintiff, resulting in damage. This requires evidence of intent and improper means used by the defendant beyond termination of the distributorship agreement itself. The trade libel claim involves allegations that false statements made by the defendant harmed the reputation or business interests of the plaintiff. The plaintiff must prove that these statements were defamatory, published to a third party, and caused damage to their business reputation or financial status. In deciding on summary judgment, I need to assess whether there are any genuine issues of material fact regarding these claims. Summary judgment is appropriate only if the moving party demonstrates no dispute as to any material fact and entitlement to judgment as a matter of law. If evidence shows that there are factual disputes requiring resolution at trial—particularly concerning intent or causation in the tort claims, or competitive impact for the antitrust claim—I must deny summary judgment. Therefore, I will evaluate whether the facts presented by both parties establish sufficient grounds for each claim beyond mere termination of a contractual relationship and whether any material facts remain disputed that necessitate further examination at trial. Enjoy! --- Happy Valentine's Day Valentine's Day is upon us once more, a day traditionally associated with love, romance, and the expression of affection. For some, it's a chance to celebrate their significant other with cards, flowers, chocolates, or even a grand gesture. For others, it might be a reminder of what they're missing or an opportunity to focus on self-love. In my household, we've always tried to make Valentine's Day special for each other and our daughters. It's not about the extravagant gifts but rather the thought and effort that goes into making someone feel loved and appreciated. This year, I plan to take my family out for a special dinner at their favorite restaurant and perhaps surprise them with some homemade treats. What about you? How do you plan to celebrate Valentine's

texts.full_text: curious about everything outside the window, cooing at birds and waving at passing bikes. I still can’t believe how fast she’s growing—four months feels like both an instant and an entire lifetime. Speaking of growth, her head seems to get heavier by the day (though she’s still the lightest, sleepiest cloud of cuteness). I’ve started noticing tiny little "mama" and "dada" sounds, which, I’m pretty sure, are just happy noises, but I’ll take them as language lessons for the future. Tomorrow’s high chair moment is already a countdown of excitement for all of us—it’s like a graduation of sorts. I just hope the floor seats we’re installing next to it don’t have to wait too long. We’re also debating starting her on rice cereal tomorrow to see how her belly handles it, but I’m keeping expectations low (read: not rushing solid food). For now, though, I’m savoring every suckled slice of watermelon, every gurgly smile at sunrise, and every time she looks at me like she finally remembers who saved her from the void of pre-birth emptiness. Here’s to you,

texts.full_text: o learn more about the backstory behind this fantastic idea. How the idea for the project came to be Liisa: I came up with the idea for this project back in January. I was drowning in a massive study load and was in the middle of one of my most stressful exam sessions so far, and felt this sudden urge to look at my old work on Flickr. When going through my old photos, multiple things became very clear to me: 1) The artistic community on Flickr was so kind and supportive, and it’s sad how this sort of authenticity and generosity had vanished from social media 2) I really missed taking self-portraits and being more creative in my approach to photography; I felt a sense of wanting to return to my roots 3) I would do so many of my old concepts differently now, and definitely much better on the technical level 4) I realized that I probably wasn’t the only one feeling like this. The importance of Flickr Liisa: Some of my very best memories were from the good old Flickr days. For example, back in 2012, I flew to the States to meet up with a bunch of complete strangers whose work I had followed on Flickr, and it ended up being the best thing ever. Those few days at the Midwest Flickr gathering were filled with so much passion, inspiration, creativity, and laughter. We went on little to no sleep, took so many photos and formed such strong bonds throughout it all. Later that year another person from Flickr came into my life – a weekend that was supposed to be a photography meetup led to falling in love instead, and now that person is my husband. It’s crazy, right?! I’m so thankful for joining Flickr about a decade ago, and for everything it’s brought into my life. texts.full_text: antiviral drugs or protocol treatments being tested. Based on data available so far, COVID-19 seems to infect men and women similarly, although men appear to have a higher risk of death than women as well as a higher risk of acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) or admission in intensive care unit (indeed, men also tend to have a higher prevalence of smoking which is related to higher expression of ACE2, the receptor for severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2) (Wenham et al. [@CR3]; Chen et al. [@CR2]; Cai [@CR1]). However, the current data stratifying by gender are not still informative, and additional observational studies are timely required to explore these aspects. Second, the current (restrictive) measures on travels, the closure of schools and relevant activities or the quarantine, which are adopted in various European countries (including Italy) in order to contain the virus infection, could have different and important repercussions on men and women separately, given that women have often needs that are largely different from those of men, in terms of health, safety or everyday activity. For instance, it is important to remember that, when compared to men, women have distinct demands on work and family, they have often primary roles as caregivers within families, or they are more frequently front-line health-care workers, thereby making them more potentially susceptible to virus infection (Wenham et al. [@CR3]). For such reasons, we strongly believe that all various measures, albeit required, should always guarantee the gender equality. In this context, in addition to *WHO Executive Board and Gender* and *COVID*-*19 Working Group* (Wenham et al. [@CR3]), we want to highlight the obligation by various health and scientific organizations to hear the voice of women in any decision making for the COVID-19. Too often, indeed, women are not adequately represented in the various organizations. Any health and scientific organization or government that does not act by respecting and promoting gender equity (even in this difficult moment) is abandoning the noble purpose to improve the human health. texts.full_text: ly that induces an acrosome reaction in spermatozoa caused Ca2+-dependent increases in 1,2-diacylglycerol and phosphatidic acid. Diacylglycerol concentrations were increased 2-fold, and phosphatidic acid concentrations were elevated more than 50% of control values within minutes after addition to spermatozoa in a calcium containing medium. This increase was blocked by the removal or chelation of extracellular Ca²⁺ or by pretreatment with n-butanol, indicating that FSG stimulates phosphatidylcholine hydrolysis via activation of phospholipase D (PLD). To further investigate this regulatory mechanism, we examined the effect of PLD inhibitors on the acrosome reaction induced by FSG. Both primary alcohols and bisindolymaleimides blocked both diacylglycerol production and spermatozoa's ability to undergo an acrosomal exocytosis upon exposure to FSG, suggesting that PLD activity is required for the initiation of this process. Our results provide evidence that sea urchin egg jelly regulates phosphatidylcholine metabolism in spermatozoa through a novel mechanism involving activation

texts.full_text: authorization mechanism for enabling or disabling reports based upon specific objects within Salesforce. When this box checked (enabled), it grants users and administrators access to create custom report types directly from that particular record type without the need of defining a separate, customized reporting structure. However your observation is correct; even if "AllowReports" checkbox isn't selected for an object in sales force you can still generate reports based on those objects. This happens because Salesforce has built-in mechanisms which allow users to create custom report types that are not tied directly with the specific record type's permission settings, but rather exist independently and could be used across various records. In essence "AllowReports" is a control for who (which profiles or roles) can build reports based on this object.
